scope: RELAY is a development-time specification and tooling layer (spec, canonical Go types, and the relay CLI). It does not route messages at runtime and holds no secrets. The threat model covers the integrity and authenticity of the tool and the evidence it produces, since downstream safety arguments depend on them.

assets.name: relay binary
assets.description: The relay CLI used as a CI conformance and reporting gate

assets.name: RELAY specification and canonical types
assets.description: spec/relay-spec.md, the embedded JSON schemas, and the Go canonical type packages that downstream implementations build against

assets.name: Conformance and safety evidence
assets.description: Outputs of relay conform/report and the safety evidence set (.fusa-reqs.json, .fusa-hara.json, .fusa-tara.json, audit pack)

threats.description: Tampered relay binary — a modified build reports PASS for a non-conformant x-Net implementation, defeating the CI release gate
threats.attackVector: Supply-chain modification of the binary or its build, or a PATH-shadowing binary substituted for relay
threats.impact: A non-conformant or unsafe implementation is released as conformant

threats.description: Spoofed capabilities/version document — a binary under test emits a forged document to pass conformance without implementing the contract
threats.attackVector: A malicious or buggy binary returns crafted JSON to relay conform/probe

threats.description: Altered specification or schema — a modified spec or embedded schema changes the conformance criteria silently
threats.attackVector: Unauthorised commit to the spec/schemas, or a modified module dependency
threats.impact: Conformance is measured against the wrong contract; safety argument invalidated

threats.description: Forged audit pack — evidence is altered after generation so an auditor certifies against tampered artifacts
threats.attackVector: Modification of audit-pack contents in transit or at rest

threats.description: Arbitrary binary execution via probe scan — relay probe --scan executes binaries on PATH, which could run an attacker-planted executable
threats.attackVector: Attacker-controlled PATH entry combined with a permissive --match
threats.impact: Code execution in the developer/CI environment

mitigations.description: Build provenance: relay reports its VCS revision and build settings (runtime/debug.BuildInfo) so the running binary can be tied back to a reviewed commit
Reproducible, dependency-free build (pure Go standard library) reduces supply-chain attack surface; SBOM (relay sbom) enumerates the module and toolchain

relay conform validates documents structurally against embedded schemas and exercises sentinel errors via golden vectors, so a forged document must actually satisfy the contract, not just look plausible
Audit pack carries a SHA-256 manifest over every bundled artifact, so any post-generation alteration is detectable
relay probe takes explicit binaries by default; --scan is opt-in and constrained by --match, and probing only runs the read-only version/capabilities commands

controls.description: Signed commits and protected main branch; releases tagged from reviewed commits with green CI (Lint, Test, DCO, CodeQL, Security, go-FuSa)
CI runs relay conform as a release gate for every x-Net implementation
CodeQL and dependency/security scanning run on every PR
Document in the tool safety manual that relay probe --scan must only be used against trusted PATH directories
